stories.title: Svitolina wins third Rome title — historic 20th WTA crown

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Elina Svitolina wins Rome for the third time. Def. Gauff 6-4, 6-7(3), 6-2.

Her 20th WTA title. First Ukrainian in the Open Era to reach 20 wins. Clay finals record: 8-0.

#TennisRecap #ItalianOpen #WTA #Svitolina

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
After winning Rome, Svitolina dedicated the title to Ukrainians watching "from bomb shelters."

Her 3rd Italian Open. 20th career title. Beat Nos. 2, 3 & 4 in four days.

She arrives at Roland Garros as the player in form. #TennisRecap #WTA

facebook.long_form:
ELINA SVITOLINA IS THE ROME CHAMPION — FOR THE THIRD TIME. 🎾

She defeated Coco Gauff 6-4, 6-7(3), 6-2 on Saturday in a final that stretched nearly three hours. After losing the second set in a tiebreak, Svitolina came back with two decisive breaks in the third to seal the title on her third match point.

This is her 20th WTA singles title. That makes her the first Ukrainian player in the Open Era to reach 20 career WTA wins. Her clay final record stands at a perfect 8-0 — she has never lost a clay court final in her entire career.

What makes this week even more extraordinary is the quality of opposition she defeated. In four consecutive match days at the Foro Italico, Svitolina defeated No. 2 Elena Rybakina, No. 4 Iga Swiatek, and No. 3 Coco Gauff. Three of the top four players in the world — on clay — in less than a week.

At the trophy ceremony, she delivered words that meant more than tennis: "I also want to thank the people in Ukraine who support me, who watch, especially from bomb shelters. The last few weeks have been very difficult for Ukraine. I feel love from everyone. Thank you. Glory to Ukraine."

She heads to Roland Garros as the No. 7 seed and the in-form player on the WTA tour. Paris, watch out.
facebook.image_caption: Elina Svitolina. Rome champion — for the third time. 20th WTA title. First Ukrainian in the Open Era to reach 20. Clay finals record: 8-0. 🎾

article.headline: Svitolina Wins Third Rome Title, Becomes First Ukrainian With 20 WTA Crowns

- Breaks won: Svitolina 6, Gauff 3

- Svitolina second-serve points won: 50% vs Gauff 38%
**Svitolina quotes (confirmed WTA official / Olympics.com):**
- "Definitely was an extremely tough battle. I'm very happy with the way I could handle my nerves, not only today but also throughout this tournament."
- "I also want to thank the people in Ukraine who support me, who watch, especially from bomb shelters. The last few weeks have been very difficult for Ukraine... I feel love from everyone. Thank you. Glory to Ukraine."

stories.title: ATP Rome Final — Sinner vs Ruud, Career Golden Masters on the line

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Today: Sinner vs. Ruud in the Rome final (not before 11 AM ET).

One win from the Career Golden Masters — Rome is the only M1000 Sinner has never won. 33-match streak. H2H: 5-0.

#TennisRecap #ItalianOpen #Sinner #ATP

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Casper Ruud is in his 5th Rome final — and has never won the title.

Lost all four previous (2020, 2021, 2022, 2024). Three Grand Slam finals, zero majors.

Today he faces Sinner (H2H 5-0). "Try not to think about the big wave." #TennisRecap

facebook.long_form:
THE BIGGEST FINAL IN JANNIK SINNER'S CAREER STARTS TODAY. 🏆

At the Foro Italico on Sunday, Sinner faces Casper Ruud for the ATP Rome title. A win today would complete the Career Golden Masters — winning every ATP Masters 1000 event at least once. Rome is the only one missing from his trophy cabinet.

Only Novak Djokovic has ever done it. If Sinner beats Ruud, he joins that company.

He arrives on a 33-match Masters 1000 winning streak — the longest in the history of the series. He is the first Italian man in the Open Era to reach back-to-back Rome finals. The entire Foro Italico will be behind him.

On the other side: Casper Ruud — in his fifth Rome final, having lost all four previous (2020, 2021, 2022, 2024). He also holds three Grand Slam runner-up finishes without a major. On Sunday, he comes in as the underdog — but he swept Darderi 6-1, 6-1 in the semifinals and has shown the form to compete.

"Try not to think about the big wave," Ruud said of facing Sinner. He knows what he's up against.

Match starts not before 5 PM local (11 AM ET). This is the one.
facebook.image_caption: Sinner vs. Ruud. Rome final today at 11 AM ET. Career Golden Masters on the line. Rome is the only M1000 Sinner has never won. 🎾

stories.title: Svitolina beats Nos. 2, 3, 4 — arrives at Roland Garros as red-hot favourite

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Svitolina at Roland Garros: No. 7 seed. Rome champion. Beat Nos. 2, 3 & 4 on clay in four days.

Clay finals record: 8-0. Only top-10 woman to win an outdoor clay title in 2026.

She's the one in form. #TennisRecap #RolandGarros #WTA

facebook.long_form:
SVITOLINA IS COMING TO PARIS — AND SHE'S IN THE FORM OF HER LIFE. 🎾

Let's put her Rome week into perspective. In four match days at the Foro Italico, she defeated:
— No. 2 Elena Rybakina (quarterfinal)
— No. 4 Iga Swiatek (semifinal)
— No. 3 Coco Gauff (final)

That's three of the world's top four players — on clay — in four days. Her clay final record is now 8-0. She has never lost a clay court final. Her ranking rises three spots to world No. 7.

She is the only top-10 woman to win an outdoor clay title in the 2026 season. She arrives at Roland Garros as the No. 7 seed and the hottest player heading to Paris.

When asked if she can win Roland Garros this year, she didn't dismiss the question. After a week like this, she doesn't have to.

Roland Garros qualifying begins Monday. Main draw starts May 25. The conversation about who wins the French Open now runs through Elina Svitolina.
facebook.image_caption: Svitolina beat Nos. 2, 3 and 4 in four days at Rome. Clay final record: 8-0. She heads to Roland Garros as the No. 7 seed and the in-form player on tour. 🎾

article.headline: From Rome to Paris: Why Svitolina Is the Player to Watch at Roland Garros

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Roland Garros qualifying begins tomorrow (Monday, May 18).

Draw ceremony: Thursday. Main draw: May 25. Alcaraz out. Sinner the favourite. Wild cards: Wawrinka (2015 champion) and Monfils in their final French Opens.

Paris is next. #TennisRecap #RolandGarros

facebook.long_form:
ROLAND GARROS WEEK ZERO STARTS NOW. 🎾

Qualifying begins Monday, May 18. The main draw ceremony is Thursday, May 21 at 2 PM Paris time. The main draw starts May 25. The French Open is no longer approaching — it is here.

MEN'S FIELD: Jannik Sinner is the overwhelming favourite — especially with Carlos Alcaraz out (wrist injury). If he wins today's Rome final, he arrives in Paris as the Career Golden Masters holder, on a record 34-match M1000 winning streak, and as the man who has never won Roland Garros. The question is whether that changes in 2026.

WOMEN'S FIELD: Elina Svitolina arrives as Rome champion, the No. 7 seed, and the in-form clay player. Coco Gauff defends her 2023 title. Iga Swiatek — four-time Roland Garros champion — will regroup after her Rome exit. Aryna Sabalenka (No. 1) targets a first French Open title.

WILD CARDS: Stan Wawrinka receives a wild card for his 21st and final French Open. The 2015 Roland Garros champion says goodbye to the tournament he won in one of the sport's great upsets. Gael Monfils also joins the field in what may be his farewell clay season.

Paris awaits.
facebook.image_caption: Roland Garros qualifying starts Monday. Main draw May 25. Sinner, Svitolina, Gauff, Swiatek, Sabalenka. The 2026 French Open is here. 🎾

stories.title: Ruud's Rome curse — 5 finals, still no title

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Five Rome finals. Zero titles.

Ruud has been to more Rome finals than almost any player alive — 2020, 2021, 2022, 2024, and now 2026.

Tonight he faces Sinner: No. 1, 33-match M1000 streak. One last chance. #TennisRecap #ItalianOpen #Ruud

facebook.long_form:
FIVE FINALS. STILL WAITING. 🎾

Casper Ruud has played more Rome finals than almost any player of his generation. He first reached the title match here in 2020. Then 2021. Then 2022. Then 2024. And today — 2026 — his fifth chance at a trophy that has always slipped away.

Beyond Rome, he holds three Grand Slam runner-up finishes without a major: Roland Garros 2022, US Open 2022, Wimbledon 2023. He is one of the best players in the world at reaching the last match of a tournament. The final step has not yet come.

Today, he faces Jannik Sinner — world No. 1, 33-match M1000 winning streak, 5-0 head-to-head against Ruud. The numbers all point against him.

"Try not to think about the big wave," Ruud said.

That line says everything. He knows what he's walking into. He's showing up anyway. Whatever happens today, that part of Casper Ruud's story is already written — and it says something admirable about the man.
facebook.image_caption: Five Rome finals. Zero titles. Today, Ruud takes on Sinner — No. 1, 5-0 H2H, on a 33-match Masters winning streak. Can he change the story? 🎾

article.headline: Ruud's Rome: Five Finals, the Longest Wait in the City
